question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: EntityService.getAccountFromAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to any account sub-route with a valid base32 alias that matches multiple entities in the database, causing getAccountFromAlias() to throw an error after performing a full alias scan, and under concurrent load with many such requests, exhaust DB connections and cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('allowances/crypto'), CryptoAllowanceController.getAccountCryptoAllowances)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/crypto with a spender.id=gte:1&spender.id=lte:999999999 range, causing CryptoAllowanceService.getAccountCryptoAllowances() to perform a full-range scan on the crypto_allowance table, holding a DB connection open for the scan duration and contributing to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.extractStakingRewardsQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with many timestamp=eq: filters, causing updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() to build a large IN clause with many timestamp values, generating a slow DB query that holds a connection open and contributes to a network partition under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: BaseController.updateConditionsAndParamsWithInValues()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with the maximum number of spender.id=eq: filters, causing updateConditionsAndParamsWithInValues() to accumulate a large invalues array that is later expanded into a large IN clause, generating a slow DB query that holds a connection open and contributes to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('nfts'), AccountController.getNftsByAccountId)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with a valid EVM address that requires a DB lookup in getEntityIdFromEvmAddress(), followed by a three-part UNION NFT query, causing three sequential DB operations per request and under concurrent load exhausting the connection pool and causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with limit=100 and a wide timestamp range, causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to return the maximum result set while holding a DB connection open for the full query duration, and under concurrent load cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: extendExpress(express.Router())] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the _once() wrapper in extendExpress.js, which prevents next() from being called more than once, to send requests that trigger an error after a DB connection is acquired but before it is released, causing connection leaks that progressively exhaust the pool and cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to all five account sub-routes (nfts, rewards, allowances/crypto, allowances/tokens, tokens) for the same account ID in rapid succession, causing up to ten sequential DB queries (two per route for EVM address resolution + data query), exhausting the connection pool and causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TokenAllowanceController.extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens with spender.id=gte:1&spender.id=lte:999999999&token.id=gte:1&token.id=lte:999999999, causing extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to generate a three-part UNION query that performs three full-range scans on the token_allowance table, holding a DB connection open for the combined duration and causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with a spender.id=gte:1&spender.id=lte:999999999 range combined with a full token.id range, causing the NFT query to perform a full-range scan with a spender filter, holding a DB connection open for an extended period and contributing to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: EntityService.getEncodedId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to any account sub-route with a valid alias string that does not exist in the database, causing getAccountIdFromAlias() to perform a full alias scan on the entity table and return null, and under concurrent load with many such requests, exhaust DB connections and cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('tokens'), TokenController.getTokenRelationships)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/tokens with limit=100 and a wide token.id range, causing TokenService.getTokenAccounts() to return the maximum result set while holding a DB connection open for the full query duration, and under concurrent load cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: BaseController.validateBounds()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with a combination of token.id and serialnumber filters that passes validateBounds() but generates a maximally complex three-part UNION query, causing the DB to perform three sequential scans and holding a connection open for the combined duration, contributing to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with a valid account ID that has a large number of staking reward transfers, causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to return a large result set that consumes significant memory and holds a DB connection open for an extended period, contributing to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: CryptoAllowanceController.getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/crypto with limit=100 and no spender.id filter, causing CryptoAllowanceService.getAccountCryptoAllowances() to return the maximum result set while holding a DB connection open for the full query duration, and under concurrent load cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('allowances/tokens'), TokenAllowanceController.getAccountTokenAllowances)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens with limit=100 and no filters, causing TokenAllowanceService.getAccountTokenAllowances() to return the maximum result set while holding a DB connection open for the full query duration, and under concurrent load cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with token.id=eq:1&serialnumber=eq:1, causing extractNftMultiUnionQuery() to generate a query for a specific NFT combination, and by sending many such requests with different token.id and serialnumber values, exhaust the DB connection pool and cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: EntityService.getEntityIdFromEvmAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to any account sub-route with a crafted EVM address that matches no entity in the database, causing getEntityIdFromEvmAddress() to perform a full scan on the entity table (WHERE evm_address = $1) and throw a NotFoundError, and under concurrent load with many such requests, exhaust DB connections and cause a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: BaseController.updateQueryFiltersWithInValues()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with the maximum number of timestamp=eq: filters, causing updateQueryFiltersWithInValues() to build a large IN clause that generates a slow DB query, holding a connection open and contributing to a network partition under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('nfts'), AccountController.getNftsByAccountId)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with a valid alias that requires a DB lookup, followed by a three-part UNION NFT query, causing two sequential DB operations per request and under concurrent load exhausting the connection pool and causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards with a valid account ID and no timestamp filter, causing StakingRewardTransferService.getRewards() to perform a full scan on the staking_reward_transfer table for that account, holding a DB connection open for the full scan duration and contributing to a network partition under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: TokenController.extractTokensRelationshipQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/tokens with many token.id=eq: filters, causing extractTokensRelationshipQuery() to build a large inConditions array that generates a slow IN-clause query, holding a DB connection open and contributing to a network partition under concurrent load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: extendExpress(express.Router())] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the async nature of the wrap() function in extendExpress.js to send a burst of requests that all trigger slow DB queries simultaneously, causing the Node.js event loop to be saturated with pending promises and the DB connection pool to be exhausted, causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with token.id=lte:999999999&serialnumber=lte:999999999, causing extractNftMultiUnionQuery() to generate a query with only upper bounds, resulting in a full-range scan on the NFT table that holds a DB connection open for an extended period and contributes to a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: BaseController.getLowerFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request to /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with only a serialnumber filter (no token.id), which would be rejected by validateSecondaryBound(), but by crafting a request that passes validation with a minimal token.id filter, cause getLowerFilters() to generate a query that scans a large portion of the NFT table, holding a DB connection open and contributing to a network partition?
